title: Casselia viliosula
intro: Casselia villosula, also known as the Casselia plant, is a type of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate conditions such as fever, colds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Casselia villosula include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in traditional folk remedies, including cough syrups and expectorants.
taxonomy: Casselia viliosula is classified in the kingdom Plantae, family Rubiaceae. The family Rubiaceae is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family.
